rulings.Case Title: HERNAN GARCIA TORRES VS. JOSHUA SHARYAR SHIDEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Pleadings By Striking Name Of Attorney And Adding Name Of Plaintiff, And To Correct Clerical Error In Clerk'S Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2024, Line 1. OTHER (NON-PARTY) LABOR COMMISSIONER's Motion To Amend Pleadings By Striking Name Of Attorney And Adding Name Of Plaintiff, And To Correct Clerical Error In Clerk'S Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc. The unopposed "motion to amend pleadings by striking name of attorney and adding name of plaintiff and to correct clerical error in clerk's judgment nunc pro tunc" is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JORGE ESTRELLA-MORENO ET AL VS. ALL NATURAL STONE BURLINGAME, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2024, Line 4. DEFENDANT CAESARSTONE LTD's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Spratt declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 435.5. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. The response to the complaint is now due October 15, 2024. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: J. DENNIS BONNEY VS. 1810 JACKSON STREET, A CONDOMINIUM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2024, Line 8. 1 - CROSS DEFENDANT J. BONNEY's DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. J. Dennis Bonney's demurrer to 1810 Jackson Street Homeowners Association's (HOA) first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is overruled. This dispute regards a condominium storage area. Bonney demurs that the FACC is wholly barred because a 2018 "amendment to the [HOA] governing documents" is effective (Memo. 2:7-13), but the FACC does not mention the amendment until the fifth of its seven counts (and the former counts do not incorporate the latter). Moreover, the HOA is not required at the pleading stage to prove the 2018 amendment ineffective. (See id.) And if a writing is attached to a complaint, plaintiff's interpretation of it must be accepted as correct on demurrer. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: J. DENNIS BONNEY VS. 1810 JACKSON STREET, A CONDOMINIUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2024, Line 8. 2 - CROSS DEFENDANT J. BONNEY's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. J. Dennis Bonney's motion to strike 1810 Jackson Street Homeowners Association's (HOA) first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is denied. Bonney says the FACC's trespass allegations are contradictory, but inconsistent allegations are permissible. (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
